title: Refresh Meshtastic protobuf portnum subset for newer upstream apps
description: Current firmware unsupported-operation catalog is complete against the repo-pinned rust/lichen-meshtastic/proto/meshtastic/portnums.proto subset. Upstream Meshtastic protobufs define additional PortNum values not yet vendored here. Refresh the vendored protobuf subset/generator expectations, then update the Zephyr adapter unsupported-operation catalog and tests to cover any newly pinned app-visible portnums.
notes:
No-hardware implementation completed for Meshtastic PortNum refresh. Source comparison: upstream meshtastic/protobufs master raw portnums.proto checked at aa53c96b79d9cb49a38e71fc2bc9c46cec1fd7c6; only LORA_OTA_APP=79 was new since the documented protobuf baseline 032b7dfd68e875c4323e6ac67590c6fc616b1714, and the local minimal subset now also vendors previously omitted app-visible values ALERT_APP=11, KEY_VERIFICATION_APP=12, REMOTE_SHELL_APP=13, STORE_FORWARD_PLUSPLUS_APP=35, NODE_STATUS_APP=36, MESH_BEACON_APP=37, POWERSTRESS_APP=74, LORAWAN_BRIDGE=75, RETICULUM_TUNNEL_APP=76, CAYENNE_APP=77, ATAK_PLUGIN_V2=78, GROUPALARM_APP=112. Updated rust/lichen-meshtastic/proto/meshtastic/portnums.proto, regenerated rust/lichen-meshtastic/src/meshtastic.rs, added LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_PORTNUMS_PROTOBUF_COMMIT, updated Zephyr adapter unsupported-operation catalog IDs/portnums/tests, and added Python drift guard coverage for catalog vs runtime unsupported portnums. ADMIN_APP=6 remains catalog-covered but is excluded from the generic unsupported-payload loop because it has parser-specific admin behavior and metadata/noop/malformed tests.

Code review evidence: explorer Pascal identified stale generated Rust and missing unsupported_portnums coverage before fixes; explorer Tesla verified upstream master aa53c96b79d9cb49a38e71fc2bc9c46cec1fd7c6 and that LORA_OTA_APP=79 is the only new value since baseline. Codereview Herschel: clean after fixes, verified upstream metadata, local proto/generated values, enum ID stability, Zephyr coverage, git diff --check and Python drift guard. Codereview Wegener: clean after fixes, checked enum IDs stable, catalog/table agreement including ADMIN_APP catalog coverage, runtime admin metadata path preserved, metadata commit. Codereview Dalton: P1 finding that including ADMIN_APP=6 in test_unsupported_operation_portnums_queue_errors caused malformed status 3 instead of unsupported status 2; fixed by separating catalog coverage from runtime generic unsupported list; no remaining findings.

Validation evidence: local git diff --check passed; python3 -m pytest -q python/tests/interface/meshtastic/test_zephyr_unsupported_portnums.py passed 1/1; cd python && python3 -m ruff check tests/interface/meshtastic/test_zephyr_unsupported_portnums.py passed; cd rust && cargo test -p lichen-meshtastic passed 27 unit tests and 0 doctests. EC2 i-08025532ad14e3b4b in us-east-2a using EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 and clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-portnum-refresh: first direct native_sim run failed 64/65 because ADMIN_APP=6 returned malformed status 3; after fix, direct command '. /mnt/lichen-zephyr/env.sh && cd /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-portnum-refresh && west build -p always -b native_sim -d build/meshtastic_adapter_native lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen && west build -d build/meshtastic_adapter_native -t run' passed 65/65. Twister native_sim command 'west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -p native_sim --outdir twister-out/meshtastic_adapter_native -c --inline-logs -x ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen' passed 1/1 configs, 65 cases, 0 warnings. Twister qemu_x86 command 'west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out/meshtastic_adapter_qemu -c --inline-logs -x ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen' passed 1/1 configs, 65 cases, 0 warnings. Initial Twister native_sim without -x failed because it picked preserved primary EBS checkout module headers; rerun with explicit clean-worktree ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES fixed that tooling issue without code changes.
EC2 cleanup completed after Bead close: ran sync and sudo umount /mnt/lichen-zephyr on i-08025532ad14e3b4b, detached EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 and waited until state=available, terminated instance i-08025532ad14e3b4b and waited until state=terminated, deleted temporary security group sg-01c17ad9114d81ccb and verified describe-security-groups returns InvalidGroup.NotFound.
close_reason: Completed: refreshed vendored Meshtastic PortNum subset against upstream aa53c96b79d9cb49a38e71fc2bc9c46cec1fd7c6, regenerated Rust protobuf output, updated Zephyr unsupported-operation catalog/tests, added drift guard coverage, and validated locally plus EC2 native_sim/qemu Twister.
